conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I can help you determine your eligibility. 

To start, do you have any children you're inquiring about for Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 5 years old. I live here in the UK and was wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for them?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know that you have Alex, who is 5 years old, and that you live in the UK.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

Do you live with Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

Just to complete my assessment, are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning you provide for their needs such as food, clothing, and general upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex and provide for all their needs - food, clothes, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the rules:

- Alex is 5 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- Alex lives with you ✓
- You're responsible for Alex and provide for their needs ✓
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

Based on the official guidance, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you
- You're responsible for Alex's care and upkeep
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16, meeting the basic age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them at home, satisfying the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all their needs including food and clothes, meeting the responsibility requirement. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, ensuring there is no duplicate claim. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 5-year-old child Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, Alex lives with them, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16, meeting the basic age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them at home, satisfying the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all their needs including food and clothes, meeting the responsibility requirement. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, ensuring there is no duplicate claim. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 5-year-old child Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, Alex lives with them, they are responsible for Alex's care and upkeep, Alex is under 16, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
